title: Investment Banker

rationales.taskAutomation: AI drafts pitch materials and runs comps quickly, but originating and closing deals depends on client trust, negotiation, and judgment under real financial stakes that resist automation.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted pitch-deck and comparable-company analysis tools exist and are improving, but nothing purpose-built has displaced core deal work.
rationales.adoption: Early adopters only: leading banks pilot AI for research and deck support, but deal execution stays fully human-run.
rationales.agenticExposure: Comp-pulling and first-draft materials are agent-ownable prep work; client-facing deal origination and negotiation are not.

tasksAtRisk: Comparable company and precedent transaction analysis
First-draft pitch book and deck creation
Financial model scaffolding and data pulls
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 41-3031 (Securities, Commodities, and Financial Services Sales Agents), p25-p75 rounded; high end reflects analyst/associate base+bonus market postings well above the broader OES median

defenseSkills.skill: Deal origination and client relationship management

insight: AI now drafts the pitch book in a fraction of the time, but the job has always really been about winning and closing the mandate, which is still a trust-and-negotiation business.
